There was a thought of AIN from levofloxacin. Her renal US was negative for hydronephrosis and she continued to make good amounts of urine despite her ARF. Initially treated with Cipro/Ceftriaxone, but discontinued as her urine cultures were negative. Given her severe prerenal state, she will need to continue to receive IV fluids on her transition back to ___ and have weekly electrolyte and Cr checks. She also had a workup for potential myeloma kidney given her history of MGUS and concern for transformation into MM. She had an SPEP (relative unchanged protein spike), UPEP (standard and a 24 hour urine collection - the 24 hour collection was done as this is more sensitive). The UPEP was not consistent with light chain myopathy. Hence, she will follow up with Dr. ___. # Lethargy/mental status: Likely a combination of uremia and hyperkalemia. This improve as her ARF and her hyperkalemia improved. # Anemia: Came in with a Hct of 36 -> down to 23.6 after fluids. Transfused 2U PRBC -> Hct bumped appropriately to 29. # supraventricular tachycardia - this was incidentally noted on routine vital check on ___. She was asymptomatic and denied chest pain, palpitations or difficulties breathing with this episode. It was a narrow complex tachycardia, however, it terminated prior to capturing on EKG and felt to be most likely atrial atchycardia vs. AVNRT. Started metoprolol 12.5mg po bid which she was tolerating well. She was monitored on telemetry for 24 hours without further incident. Her EKG was checked and unchanged from prior. # MGUS - followed by heme/onc outpt, currently has labs drawn on weekly basis to follow counts; no evidence of multiple myeloma at this time. Currently, her HCT is much higher than her baseline of upper ___. UPEP and SPEP were not significantly changed, hence concern for myeloma was low. Amyloid is also a consideration as MGUS can transition to amyloid as well. No comment on REnal US of consistency of kidneys. She was seen by Dr. ___ in house and will continue to be seen as an outpatient. She has a follow up in the next 2 weeks with Dr. ___. # ITP - recently complete course of prednisone for exacerbation; currently platelets are above baseline and stable. F/up with Dr. ___ # Hypothyroidism - continued levothyroxine # Depression - continued citalopram # Fluid status: to continue to 1L of NS per day on discharge until kidney function returns to normal # Medications held: to restart bicarbonate and claritin per discretion of PCP # Dispo: return to ___ at ___. She should have bloodwork within one week to check Chem7 and CBC. These results should be sent to on-call physician.
summary: You were admitted with elevated potassium levels and kidney failure. These were treated with medications and intravenous fluids. Your potassium levels returned to normal and your kidney function has returned to near normal levels. We also did an evaluation to ensure that mutiple myeloma was not causing your kidney failure - we did not find any evidence of this. You will need to have your electrolytes and your kidney function checked at least weekly. We recommend for the next 3 days to have your kidney function checked daily to ensure that it continues to improve. We recommend that you receive intravenous fluids (NS at 75cc/hr) for 1L daily until your kidney function returns to normal.

text: Brief Hospital Course: ___ with h/o recurrent pneumonia, asplenia ___ trauma in ___, CVA w/ L-sided deficits, depression, GERD presents with SOB, fever, and cough # Sepsis due to aspiration Pneumonia: He was initially hypotensive to ___ requiring pressors and was admitted to the MICU. He was treated with vancomycin, Zosyn and azithromycin with improvement. He was transferred to the floor the next day. His oxygenation improved and he was saturating 94% on room air. Several days after transfer to the floor he had another aspiration event requiring 2L of O2 to maintain saturations in the low ___ which improved with time. CT scan showed multifocal pneumonia involving all lobes. Speech and swallow evaluated the patient and determined that he is actively aspirating and that this has worsened since ___. He underwent swallowing therapy during his stay and should continue to have swallowing therapy as an outpatient. Additionally, he would benefit from GI follow up for decreased ___ which contributes to reflux and aspiration as well as ENT for vocal cord dysfunction which makes his cough weak and increases his risk for pneumonia. # ___: Creatinine was initially over 2 but improved with IV fluids. After transfer to the floor, his creatinine remained at baseline around 0.8 until 24 hours after CT scan with contrast when he developed acute renal failure likely due to contrast induced nephropathy. He also continued to have diarrhea which was c.diff negative so he was given IV fluids. His creatinine was 2.1 on the day of discharge and will be followed at rehab. Vancomycin will be dosed accordingly. Gabapentin was renally dosed and lisinopril was held until renal function improves. Verbal signout given to NP on call at ___ with these instructions as well. # Diarrhea: Initially concern for c.diff but assay was negative. Attributed to antibiotic associated diarrhea, particularly given history of diarrhea with abx in the past. Patient was given loperamide prn and IVF. # Hypotension: Fluid-responsive in ER w/ ___ - he was transiently on levophed, which was weaned when he arrived in the ICU. Likely ___ early sepsis and hypovolemia. Lactate normalized with IVF. # Chronic pain: MS ___ was reduced to 30 mg q8h in renal failure but increased at patient request due to pain and lack of adverse effects at higher dose. # Hypertension: Lisinopril was held during hospitalization due to ___. It should be restarted once kidney function improves. # Depression: The patient was continued on wellbutrin, clonazepam, methylphenidate, sertraline. TRANSITIONAL ISSUES: FULL CODE -patient will need daily creatinine check and dosing of vancomycin per levels, goal level ___ -antibiotics are to continue through ___ -lisinopril was stopped during admission and should be restarted once renal function improves -gabapentin dose was reduced due to ___ and ___ be returned to home dose once creatinine normalizes -patient should have GI and ENT appointments as an outpatient for reduction of aspiration risk and should continue with outpatient swallow therapy at ___
summary: You were admitted for pneumonia. You were treated with antibiotics, and your illness improved. You underwent a CT scan to look for an anatomical reason for your recurrent pneumonias, which revealed a serious lung infection but no obvious mass or abscess. You underwent a video swallow study that showed that you are aspirating (meaning that your oral and gastric secretions are getting into your lungs), which is putting you at risk for these recurrent pneumonias. You also developed kidney injury from contrast given to you for your CAT scan and dehydration related to your diarrhea, which was felt to be the effect of antibitoics, and not a diarrhea infection). You were given IV fluids and your kidney function is expected to improve with time. Your pain medication was decreased to prevent sedation with your kidney dysfunction, but was increased back to your home dose before discharge, as you were tolerating it fine. You were discharged to finish your course of antibiotics.
